criteria.evidence: meta-tests/test-git.bats exists (471 lines), starts with '#!/usr/bin/env bats' shebang on line 1, and loads helper via "load 'helpers/test-helper'" on line 8.
Ran 'bats meta-tests/test-git.bats': exit code 0, all 25 tests show 'ok', no 'not ok' lines.
Ran 'bash meta-tests/run.sh': exit code 0, 83 tests total (25 git + 58 prior), all pass, no regressions.

25 @test blocks found (>= 20 required). All 6 git.sh functions covered: git_create_harness_branch (4), git_create_sprint_branch (4), git_merge_sprint (6), git_fail_sprint_attempt (4), git_commit_harness_state (3), generate_pr_body (4).
Test at line 27 calls git_create_harness_branch 'my-project' and asserts 'harness/my-project' branch exists via git rev-parse --verify. Test passes.
Test at line 37 captures output via 'run --separate-stderr git_create_harness_branch "test-slug"' and asserts output == 'harness/test-slug'. Test passes.

Test at line 46 calls git_create_harness_branch 'idempotent-test' twice (with checkout to main between calls), asserts second call exits 0, returns correct branch name, and repo is on correct branch. Test passes.
Test at line 81 calls git_create_sprint_branch 'harness/my-project' 3 and verifies branch 'harness/my-project-sprint-03' exists (zero-padded). Test passes.
Test at line 92 checks output == 'harness/my-project-sprint-05' and asserts current branch is the sprint branch after the call. Test passes.

Test at line 107 pre-creates stale 'harness/my-project-sprint-02' branch with a commit, advances harness HEAD, calls git_create_sprint_branch, and asserts the new sprint branch HEAD matches the harness branch HEAD (not the stale SHA). Test passes.

Test at line 151 sets up harness and sprint branches with a commit, calls git_merge_sprint, verifies current branch is 'harness/my-project', and verifies merge commit message contains 'harness(sprint-01)' via git log --oneline. Test passes.
Test at line 174 calls git_merge_sprint and verifies tag 'harness/sprint-01/pass' exists via git rev-parse --verify. Also asserts tag SHA equals HEAD SHA (tag points to merge commit). Test passes.
Test at line 195 calls git_merge_sprint then runs 'git rev-parse --verify harness/my-project-sprint-01' and asserts it fails (status != 0), confirming sprint branch is deleted. Test passes.
Test at line 211 captures output of git_merge_sprint via --separate-stderr, gets HEAD SHA, and asserts output contains the merge SHA via glob match. Test passes.

Test at line 230 creates uncommitted file 'uncommitted.txt' on sprint branch, calls git_merge_sprint, then verifies the file exists and contains expected content on the harness branch after merge. Test passes.
Test at line 269 sets up sprint branch with a commit, calls git_fail_sprint_attempt with attempt=2, and verifies tag 'harness/sprint-01/attempt-2' exists via git rev-parse --verify. Test passes.

Test at line 284 calls git_fail_sprint_attempt, asserts current branch is 'harness/my-project' (harness branch), and asserts sprint branch no longer exists (git rev-parse --verify fails). Test passes.

Test at line 305 creates a tracked file, commits it, then modifies it (dirty working tree), calls git_fail_sprint_attempt, asserts exit 0, and verifies git status --porcelain is empty (clean working directory). Test passes.

Test at line 355 modifies harness-state/config.json, calls git_commit_harness_state with message 'harness(sprint-01): update config', and verifies git log -1 shows the expected commit message. Test passes.
Test at line 369 records commit count before, calls git_commit_harness_state with no pending changes, and asserts commit count is unchanged. Test passes.

Test at line 382 creates initial tracked config.json, modifies it, adds new untracked new-file.json, calls git_commit_harness_state, and verifies both files appear in the commit via git diff-tree. Test passes.

Test at line 409 installs config-valid.json and sprint-plan-3sprint.json fixtures, calls generate_pr_body, and asserts output contains the exact table header '| Sprint | Name | Status | Criteria | Pass | Fail | Attempts | Cost |'. Test passes.
Test at line 423 installs eval-report-pass.json fixture for sprint-01, calls generate_pr_body, and asserts output contains 'PASS'. Test passes.
Test at line 440 installs eval report only for sprint 1 (plan has 3 sprints), calls generate_pr_body, and asserts output contains 'pending' for sprints without reports. Test passes.

Test at line 457 calls generate_pr_body with config-valid.json fixture and asserts output contains 'opus', 'reset', and 'web-app' (the model, context strategy, and project type values from the fixture). Test passes.

The custom setup() function (lines 10-21) creates a fresh TEST_TEMP_DIR via mktemp -d and cd's into it for every test. Every git-dependent test calls init_test_repo which does 'git init . -q -b main'. No test references branches, tags, or state from another test.

All 25 test names verified via regex against pattern '^[a-z_]+: '. All follow 'function_name: descriptive behavior' convention (e.g., 'git_create_harness_branch: creates a harness/{slug} branch from main').
Lines 19-20 use source_harness_lib 'utils.sh' and source_harness_lib 'git.sh'. Only mention of 'harness/lib/git.sh' is in a comment on line 2, not a source path. No hardcoded source paths.

regression.evidence: Full suite via 'bash meta-tests/run.sh' passes with 83 tests (25 new git tests + 8 test-helper + 27 test-utils-pure + 23 test-utils-state), exit code 0. No regressions detected.

correctness.notes: All 25 tests pass. Full suite of 83 tests passes with no failures.

testCoverage.notes: All 6 git.sh functions covered. 25 tests exceed the 20 minimum. Covers success paths, edge cases (idempotency, stale branches, dirty working tree, no-op commits), and output verification. Minor observation: git.sh creates lightweight tags (git tag without -a), which the tests correctly verify, though contract description says 'annotated tag'.

codeQuality.notes: Consistent naming convention. Well-organized with section headers. Proper use of '--separate-stderr' for stdout isolation. Pragmatic workarounds for bash 3.2 limitations (glob match instead of negative array index). No linting issues.

testIsolation.notes: Every test gets a fresh temp directory and git repo. No inter-test dependencies. teardown() from test-helper properly cleans up temp dirs.

robustness.notes: Tests handle edge cases well: stale branch replacement, dirty working trees, multiple failed attempts, no-op commits. Works correctly with macOS bash 3.2 constraints.

notes: Excellent implementation. All 28 criteria pass. 25 well-structured, isolated tests covering all 6 git.sh functions with proper assertions. The full suite of 83 tests passes with no regressions. Minor observation: the annotated tag description in C4-12 does not match the lightweight tag created by git.sh, but the test correctly verifies actual behavior.
